equiml
  • audit
  • View page source

audit

equiml.audit(y_true, y_pred, sensitive, metrics='all', pred_type=None)[source]

Audit a model for fairness across sensitive groups.

Parameters:

  • y_true (np.ndarray) – Ground truth labels.

  • y_pred (np.ndarray) – Model predictions — discrete labels {0, 1} or probabilities [0, 1]. Type is detected automatically unless pred_type is specified.

  • sensitive (np.ndarray or list[np.ndarray]) – Sensitive attribute(s). Pass a single np.ndarray for a single attribute, or a list of np.ndarray for intersectional analysis.

  • metrics (str or list, default='all') – Metrics to compute. Options: - ‘all’: compute all metrics compatible with the detected pred_type. - list of strings: e.g. [‘demographic_parity’, ‘auc_parity’]. - list of FairnessMetric instances: for custom metrics. - mixed list of strings and FairnessMetric instances.

  • pred_type (str or None, default=None) – Override automatic detection. Must be ‘labels’ or ‘proba’.

Returns:

Object containing computed metrics, detected pred_type, and groups.

Return type:

AuditResult

Examples

>>> import numpy as np
>>> from equiml import audit
>>> y_true = np.array([1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 0])
>>> y_pred = np.array([1, 0, 0, 0, 1, 1])
>>> sensitive = np.array([0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1])
>>> result = audit(y_true, y_pred, sensitive)
>>> result.metrics["demographic_parity"].value
